Cursor 发布了一份开发者报告，里面有些数据很有意思

挑几个比较重要的点来说：

头部用户的 AI 代码产出、token 消耗、PR 合并量都远高于中位数，而且差距还在扩大。

AI 写代码前读得越来越多，input/output token ratio 大幅上升。真正贵是理解一个代码库和任务。

缓存变得非常重要，如果每次 https://t.co/OFfYL69ZAW

Reconstructing software engineering around AI is going to take work (even as the ability of AI to code increases at a rapid rate).

Organizations are ideally spending tokens for two things:
1) building stuff
2) experiments to figure out best practices (which involves failure)

Claude Opus 4.8 just dropped. Anthropic says it's 4x less likely to let flawed code slip through than 4.7.

But the model is only half of it. The other half is how you prompt it.

Here are 7 Claude Code prompts the best builders already run on repeat. Steal all 7: 👇 https://t.co/bBX5KXnmCM

1. Plan-First Lock

Before any feature, paste this:

"Before writing any code, explore the relevant files and explain your plan for [feature]. List the files you'll change, the approach for each, and any edge cases you see. Do not write a single line until I approve the plan."

2. The Grill

After Claude says it's done, don't trust it. Make it defend the work:

"Grill me on these changes. Diff your work against main, find every assumption you made, every edge case you didn't handle, and every place this could break in production. Don't open a PR until

3. Hands-Free Debugger

When something breaks, stop copy-pasting error logs back and forth:

"Run the failing test or command yourself. Read the actual error output, form a hypothesis about the root cause, fix it, then re-run to confirm the fix worked. Repeat until the test

4. Scrap-and-Redo

When Claude gives you a working but ugly fix, don't accept mediocre:

"Knowing everything you know now after solving this, throw away the current implementation and rewrite it as the clean, elegant solution you'd write if you'd understood the problem from the

5. Interview

For anything ambiguous, don't write the full spec yourself. Make Claude extract it:

"I want to build [rough idea]. Before we start, interview me. Ask me one question at a time about requirements, constraints, edge cases, and what done looks like. Keep asking until

7. Standing Orders

Set this up once and every future session inherits it. Create a CLAUDE. md with:

"Project conventions Claude must follow:
- Stack and key libraries: [list]
- What can never change: [constraints]
- How we test: [command + what passing looks like]
- Code style

If you found this useful, check this out too.

Turn Claude into 20+ different specialists for marketing & business.

Install real expertise, not just prompts.

Get my Claude skills bundle 👇
https://t.co/4GX8faMyEP

有点意思的分享：Data for AI：明其所耗，知其所因！让每一分 Token 消耗都可量化的全栈实践

https://t.co/EYUaEIxCIu

关于我当初对 $RPI 的投资想法是如何一步步演变的，这篇文章写得太精彩了！

中文圈的朋友真的很擅长讲故事我非常乐意让更多人看到这类好文

尤其是，通过这些复盘故事大家可以更好地了解我在构思原创做多逻辑时的思考过程。
